name: MetaVia Inc.
business: MetaVia Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused primarily on developing novel pharmaceuticals to treat cardiometabolic diseases. We have two programs focused primarily on the treatment of metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH) and obesity. Vanoglipel (DA-1241) is a novel GPR119 agonist with development optionality as a standalone or combination therapy for both MASH and Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, and DA-1726 is a novel oxyntomodulin analog functioning as a GLP-1 receptor and glucagon receptor dual agonist for the treatment of obesity.
sellsTo: Patients with MASH, type 2 diabetes, and obesity

exposures.sentence: We have two programs focused primarily on the treatment of metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis ( MASH ) and obesity.
Announced positive AI-modeling results from the ongoing collaboration with Syntekabio, Inc., an AI-driven drug discovery company, leveraging their proprietary DeepMatcher platform.

catalysts.event: Completed last patient last visit for Phase 2a clinical trial of vanoglipel
catalysts.sentence: In November 2024, we completed the last patient last visit for the Phase 2a clinical trial.

catalysts.event: Announced positive topline 16-week results from Phase 2a clinical trial
catalysts.sentence: In December 2024, we announced positive topline 16-week results from the two-part Phase 2a clinical trial in patients with presumed MASH.
In May 2025, we presented the topline 16-week results from the two-part Phase 2a clinical trial at the European Association for the Study of the Liver ( EASL ) 2025.

catalysts.event: Presented sub-group analysis at AASLD The Liver Meeting 2025
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, we presented sub-group analysis on glucose control in prediabetes and diabetes subjects and additional data on inflammation and lipidomic profiles at the American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases ( AASLD ) The Liver Meeting 2025.

catalysts.event: Finalize CSR of Phase 2a clinical trial
catalysts.sentence: We are expecting to finalize the Clinical Study Report ( CSR ) of the Phase 2a clinical trial in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Announced positive topline data from SAD Part 1 of Phase 1 for DA-1726
catalysts.sentence: In September 2024, we announced positive topline data from the single ascending dose ( SAD ) Part 1 of our Phase 1 clinical trial evaluating DA-1726

catalysts.event: Announced positive topline data from MAD Part 2 up to 32 mg for DA-1726
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, we announced positive topline data from our up to 32 mg, MAD cohort Part 2 of the Phase 1 clinical trial.

catalysts.event: Began enrollment for higher-dose MAD cohort for DA-1726
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, we began enrollment for a higher-dose MAD cohort to assess the maximum tolerated dose and fully realize DA-1726 s potential

catalysts.event: Dosed first patient in 48 mg MAD cohort for DA-1726
catalysts.sentence: We dosed the first patient in the non-titrated 48 mg, MAD cohort of our Phase 1 clinical trial in July 2025

catalysts.event: Administered fifth dose for first patient in 48 mg MAD cohort
catalysts.sentence: Also, in August 2025, we administered the fifth dose for the first patient in the 48 mg, MAD cohort of our Phase 1 clinical trial.

catalysts.event: Announced positive topline data from non-titrated 48 mg MAD cohort
catalysts.sentence: On January 5, 2026, we announced positive topline data from our non-titrated 48 mg, MAD cohort 5 Part 2 of the Phase 1 clinical trial.

catalysts.event: Start Part 3a and Part 3b of Phase 1 clinical trial for DA-1726
catalysts.sentence: We are planning to start Part 3a and Part 3b of the Phase 1 clinical trial in April 2026.

catalysts.event: Data readout for Part 3a and Part 3b of Phase 1 clinical trial
catalysts.sentence: The data readout for both Part 3a and Part 3b is planned for the fourth quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Received IRB approval for Phase 1 Part 3 titration study of DA-1726
catalysts.sentence: March 2026: Received IRB approval from Clinical Pharmacology of Miami for the Phase 1 Part 3 16-week titration study of DA-1726, enabling higher-dose evaluation in obese, otherwise healthy adults.

tamClaims.claim: MASLD affects approximately 30% of the global population, and MASH develops in approximately 12% to 14% of MASLD patients.
tamClaims.sentence: The prevalence of MASLD, which affects approximately 30% of the global population, and MASH, which develops in approximately 12% to 14% of MASLD patients, is growing

tamClaims.claim: Over the past three decades, the global prevalence of MASLD has grown substantially from 17.6% (1990) to over 30% (2024).
tamClaims.sentence: Over the past three decades, the global prevalence of MASLD has grown substantially from 17.6% (1990) to over 30% (2024), with an average increase of about 0.7% to 0.8% annually.

tamClaims.claim: In 2022, there were 2.5 billion adults, approximately 43% overweight and 16% obese.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the World Health Organization ( WHO ), in 2022, there were 2.5 billion adults (18 years and older), and approximately 43% of all adults were overweight and 890 million, or approximately 16%, were obese.

tamClaims.claim: More than one billion people in the world are now obese and nearly three billion people are either overweight or obese.
tamClaims.sentence: In 2024, the NCD Risk Factor Collaboration published findings that estimate that more than one billion people in the world are now obese and nearly three billion people are either overweight or obese.
